responses.default: Based on your calendar and spending patterns, I'd recommend reviewing your upcoming social events—they often drive 30% of discretionary spend. Want me to suggest a weekly cap?
responses.overspend: Your overspend this month is mainly from 3 areas: dining out (+$120 vs last month), ride-share during busy work weeks (+$45), and a one-off gift. I can help you set category caps for next month.
responses.afford_weekend: With $588 left in your budget and ~$156 predicted for the weekend (brunch + one dinner), you're in good shape. Just avoid extra Uber or impulse shopping to stay on track.
responses.trigger: Your biggest spending triggers are: 1) Calendar events with 'dinner' or 'lunch', 2) Busy work weeks (more Uber), 3) Weekend social plans. We can set alerts before high-spend events.
responses.savings_goal: Given your current burn rate, saving $200 by end of March is doable. Focus on: cooking 2 weekend meals, skipping 2 café runs, and pausing one unused subscription. I can turn these into a challenge.
